Additional impairments were recorded for computer software and hardware no longer being utilized</div> ($<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AssetImpairmentCharges" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_ComputerSoftwareAndHardwareMemberusgaapPropertyPlantAndEquipmentByTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">191</ix:nonFraction>) and an amended customer contract ($<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AssetImpairmentCharges" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_CustomerContractsMemberusgaapFiniteLivedIntangibleAssetsByMajorClassAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">27</ix:nonFraction>)<div style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, serif; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;text-indent: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;text-indent: 0px;;display:inline;">no longer in use. The 2021 impairment loss of<div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div></div><div style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, serif; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;text-indent: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">$<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AssetImpairmentCharges" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021_RenewableNaturalGasAndRenewableElectricityGenerationMemberusgaapStatementBusinessSegmentsAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">1,191</ix:nonFraction><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;text-indent: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;text-indent: 0px;;display:inline;"></div></div></div>was due to the closure of two REG Sites and also the disposal of machinery at one RNG site. The Company recorded an impairment loss of $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AssetImpairmentCharges" contextRef="P01_01_2020To12_31_2020_PicoEnergyLlcMemberusgaapBusinessAcquisitionAxis_RenewableNaturalGasMemberusgaapStatementBusinessSegmentsAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">278</ix:nonFraction> for the year ended December&#160;31, 2020 in the Renewable Electricity Generation <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">segment</div>. The impairment loss was due to a termination of a development agreement related to the acquisition of Pico Energy, LLC (&#8220;Pico&#8221;). </div><div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Impairment loss was recorded under Operating expenses within the Consolidated Statements of Operations for the years ended December&#160;31, 2022, 2021, and 2020. </div></div></div></ix:continuation><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69697774"> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;;text-indent: 0px;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">NOTE 4&#8212;REVENUES FROM CONTRACTS WITH CUSTOMERS </div></div></div></div> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The Company&#8217;s revenues are comprised of renewable energy and related Environmental attribute sales provided under long-term contracts with its customers. All revenue is recognized when (or as) the Company satisfies its performance obligation(s) under the contract (either implicit or explicit) by transferring the promised product or service to its customer either when (or as) its customer obtains control of the product or service. A performance obligation is a promise in a contract to transfer a distinct product or service to a customer. A contract&#8217;s transaction price is allocated to each distinct performance obligation. The Company allocates the contract&#8217;s transaction price to each performance obligation using the product&#8217;s observable market standalone selling price for each distinct product in the contract. </div></div></div> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Revenue is measured as the amount of consideration the Company expects to receive in exchange for transferring its products or services. As such, revenue is recorded net of allowances and customer discounts as well as net of transportation and gathering costs incurred by the customer following the transfer of control of the commodities sold. To the extent applicable, sales, value add and other taxes collected from customers and remitted to governmental authorities are accounted for on a net (excluded from revenues) basis. </div></div> <br/></div> </ix:nonNumeric></div></div> </div></div></div> <div><div style="background-color:white;display: inline;"><div style="text-align:center"><div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto"><ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69697774" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69697774_1"><div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The Company&#8217;s performance obligations related to the sale of renewable energy (i.e. RNG and Renewable Electricity) are generally satisfied over time. Revenue related to the sale of renewable energy is generally recognized over time using an output based upon the product quantity delivered to the customer. This measure is used to best depict the Company&#8217;s performance to date under the terms of the contract. Revenue from products transferred to customers over time accounted for approximately<div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div></div><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_CustomerConcentrationRiskMemberusgaapConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis_RenewableEnergyMembersrtProductOrServiceAxis_RenewableNaturalGasAndRenewableElectricityGenerationMemberusgaapStatementBusinessSegmentsAxis_RevenueFromContractWithCustomerSegmentBenchmarkMemberusgaapConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis_TransferredOverTimeMemberusgaapTimingOfTransferOfGoodOrServiceAxis" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">29</ix:nonFraction><div style="font-size: 10pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The nature of the Company&#8217;s long-term contracts may give rise to several types of variable consideration, such as periodic price increases. This variable consideration is outside of the Company&#8217;s influence as the variable consideration is dictated by the market. Therefore, the variable consideration associated with the long-term contracts is considered fully constrained. </div><div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company&#8217;s performance obligations related to the sale of Environmental Attributes are generally satisfied at a point in time and were approximately <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_ElectricEnvironmentalAttributesMembersrtProductOrServiceAxis_ProductConcentrationRiskMemberusgaapConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis_RevenueFromContractWithCustomerProductAndServiceBenchmarkMemberusgaapConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis_TransferredAtPointInTimeMemberusgaapTimingOfTransferOfGoodOrServiceAxis" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">71</ix:nonFraction>%, <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021_ElectricEnvironmentalAttributesMembersrtProductOrServiceAxis_ProductConcentrationRiskMemberusgaapConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis_RevenueFromContractWithCustomerProductAndServiceBenchmarkMemberusgaapConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis_TransferredAtPointInTimeMemberusgaapTimingOfTransferOfGoodOrServiceAxis" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">72</ix:nonFraction>% and <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" contextRef="P01_01_2020To12_31_2020_ElectricEnvironmentalAttributesMembersrtProductOrServiceAxis_ProductConcentrationRiskMemberusgaapConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis_RevenueFromContractWithCustomerProductAndServiceBenchmarkMemberusgaapConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis_TransferredAtPointInTimeMemberusgaapTimingOfTransferOfGoodOrServiceAxis" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">68</ix:nonFraction>% <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; The Company recognizes Environmental Attribute revenue at the point in time in which the customer obtains control of the Environmental Attributes, which is generally when the title of the Environmental Attribute passes to the customer upon delivery. In limited cases, title does not transfer to the customer and revenue is not recognized until the customer has accepted the Environmental </div></div><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Attributes. The Company&#8217;s performance obligations under its counterparty sharing agreements are generally satisfied at a point in time when the earnings process is completed by the counterparty. <td>&#160;</td></tr></table></ix:continuation> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;;text-indent: 0px;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Practical expedients </div></div><div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company elected to recognize the sale of the gas and electric commodities using the right to invoice practical expedient. The Company determined that the amounts invoiced to customers correspond directly with the value to customers and the Company&#8217;s satisfaction of the performance obligations to date. Furthermore, with the election of the right to invoice practical expedient, the Company also elects to omit disclosures on the remaining, or unsatisfied performance obligations since the revenue recognized corresponds to the amount that the Company has the right to invoice. </div></ix:continuation><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:AccountsAndNontradeReceivableTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true"> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;;text-indent: 0px;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">NOTE 5&#8212;ACCOUNTS AND OTHER RECEIVABLES </div></div><div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company extends credit based upon an evaluation of the customer&#8217;s financial condition. Credit terms are consistent with industry standards and practices. The Company concluded that it met the criteria under applicable guidance for a long-lived asset to be held for sale, and accordingly reclassified the emissions allowances of $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AssetsHeldForSaleNotPartOfDisposalGroupCurrent" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2021_NitrogenOxidenoxCreditsMemberusgaapLongLivedAssetsHeldForSaleByAssetTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">777</ix:nonFraction> as current assets held for sale on the Consolidated Balance Sheet at December&#160;31, 2021. The Company estimated the fair value of these assets and concluded that the fair value exceeded the carrying value and <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOfLongLivedAssetsToBeDisposedOf" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen">no</ix:nonFraction> impairment was recorded by the Company for the year ended December&#160;31, 2021. In March 2022, the NOx emissions allowances credits were sold for $<ix:nonFraction name="mntk:SaleOfAllowancesCredit" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">1,088</ix:nonFraction>. top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">2022. </div></div></div><div style="font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 0pt; <td>&#160;</td></tr></table></ix:nonNumeric> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;;text-indent: 0px;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Amended Credit Agreement </div></div></div></div><div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">On December&#160;12, 2018, Montauk Energy Holdings LLC (&#8220;MEH&#8221;) entered into the Second Amended and Restated Revolving Credit and Term Loan Agreement (as amended, &#8220;Credit Agreement&#8221;), by and among MEH, the financial institutions from time to time party thereto as lenders and Comerica Bank, as the administrative agent, sole lead arranger and sole bookrunner (&#8220;Comerica&#8221;). The Credit Agreement (i)&#160;amended and restated in its entirety MEH&#8217;s prior revolving credit and term loan facility, dated as of August&#160;4, 2017, as amended, with Comerica and certain other financial institutions and (ii)&#160;replaced in its entirety the prior credit agreement, dated as of August&#160;4, 2017, as amended, between Comerica and Bowerman Power LFG, LLC, a wholly-owned subsidiary of MEH. </div></div></div><div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">On March&#160;21, 2019, MEH entered into the first amendment to the Credit Agreement (the &#8220;First Amendment&#8221;), which clarified a variety of terms, definitions and calculations in the Credit Agreement. The Credit Agreement requires the Company to maintain customary affirmative and negative covenants, including certain financial covenants, which are measured at the end of each fiscal quarter. </div></div></div><div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">On August&#160;28, 2019, the Company received a temporary waiver for an anticipated Event of Default (as defined in the Credit Agreement) for the consecutive three-month period ended on August&#160;31, 2019 (the &#8220;Specified Event of Default&#8221;). The Specified Event of Default was waived through October&#160;1, 2019. On September&#160;12, 2019, the Company entered into the Second Amendment. Among other matters, the Second Amendment redefined the Fixed Charge Coverage Ratio (as defined in the Credit Agreement), reduced the </div></div></div><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none; text-decoration: none;;display:inline;"> </div></div> <div style="margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; font-size: 8pt;"><ix:exclude>&#160;</ix:exclude></div><div style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;text-align:center"><ix:exclude>-<div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">89</div>-</ix:exclude></div></ix:nonNumeric></div></div><ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69698302" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698302_1"><ix:exclude><div style="margin-top:1em; margin-bottom:0em; page-break-before:always"></div></ix:exclude></ix:continuation><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698302_1" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698302_2"><ix:exclude><hr style="color:#999999;height:3px;width:100%"/></ix:exclude></ix:continuation><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698302_2" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698302_3"><ix:exclude> <div style="font-size: .83em;margin-top : 1.67em;margin-bottom : 1.67em;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div></ix:exclude></ix:continuation></div></div></div> <div> <div style="background-color:white;display: inline;"> <div style="text-align:center"> <div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto"><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698302_3"> <div style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">commitments under the revolving credit facility to $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" contextRef="PAsOn09_12_2019_MehMemberusgaapLineOfCreditFacilityAxis_SecondAmendmentMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">80,000</ix:nonFraction>, redefined the Total Leverage Ratio (as defined in the Credit Agreement) and eliminated the RIN Floor (as defined in the Second Amendment) as an Event of Default. In connection with the Second Amendment, the Company paid down the outstanding term loan by $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:RepaymentsOfLinesOfCredit" contextRef="P09_12_2019To09_12_2019_MehMemberusgaapLineOfCreditFacilityAxis_SecondAmendmentMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">38,250</ix:nonFraction> and the resulting quarterly principal installments were reduced to $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentPeriodicPaymentPrincipal" contextRef="P09_12_2019To09_12_2019_MehMemberusgaapLineOfCreditFacilityAxis_TermLoansMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">2,500</ix:nonFraction>. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In connection with the completion of the Reorganization Transactions and the IPO, the Company entered into the third amendment to the Credit Agreement (the &#8220;Third Amendment&#8221;). This amendment permitted the Change of Control provisions, as defined in the underlying agreement, to permit the Reorganization Transactions and IPO to be completed. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">On December&#160;21, 2021, MEH entered into the Fourth Amendment to the Second Amended and Restated Revolving Credit and Term Loan Agreement. The current credit agreement, which is secured by a lien on substantially all assets of the Company and certain of its subsidiaries, provides for a $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" contextRef="PAsOn12_21_2021_MehMemberusgaapLineOfCreditFacilityAxis_SecondAmendmentMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">80,000</ix:nonFraction> term loan and a $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:RepaymentsOfLinesOfCredit" contextRef="P12_21_2021To12_21_2021_MehMemberusgaapLineOfCreditFacilityAxis_SecondAmendmentMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">120,000</ix:nonFraction> revolving credit facility. The term loan amortizes in quarterly installments of $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentPeriodicPaymentPrincipal" contextRef="P12_21_2021To12_21_2021_MehMemberusgaapLineOfCreditFacilityAxis_SecondAmendmentAndRestatedRevolvingCreditAndTermLoanAgreementMemberMNTKAgreementAxis_TermLoansMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis_ThroughTwoThousandAndTwentyFourMemberMNTKPeriodAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">2,000</ix:nonFraction> through 2024, then increases to $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LongTermDebt" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2021_FromTwoThousandAndTwentyFiveAndUptoTwoThousandAndTwetnySixMemberMNTKPeriodAxis_MehMemberusgaapLineOfCreditFacilityAxis_SecondAmendmentAndRestatedRevolvingCreditAndTermLoanAgreementMemberMNTKAgreementAxis_TermLoansMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">3,000</ix:nonFraction> from 2025 to 2026 with a final payment of $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentPeriodicPaymentTermsBalloonPaymentToBePaid" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2021_FromTwoThousandAndTwentyFiveAndUptoTwoThousandAndTwetnySixMemberMNTKPeriodAxis_MehMemberusgaapLineOfCreditFacilityAxis_SecondAmendmentAndRestatedRevolvingCreditAndTermLoanAgreementMemberMNTKAgreementAxis_TermLoansMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">32,000</ix:nonFraction> in late 2026. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As of December&#160;31, 2022, $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LongTermDebt" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022_TermLoansMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">72,000</ix:nonFraction> was outstanding under the term loan. In addition, the Company had $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LettersOfCreditOutstandingAmount" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022_LetterOfCreditMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">3,905</ix:nonFraction> of outstanding letters of credit as of December&#160;31, 2022. Amounts available under the revolving credit facility are reduced by any amounts outstanding under letters of credit. As of December&#160;31, 2022, the Company&#8217;s capacity available for borrowing under the revolving credit facility was $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityCurrentBorrowingCapacity" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022_RevolvingCreditFacilityMemberusgaapCreditFacilityAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">116,095</ix:nonFraction>. Borrowings of the term loan&#160;and revolving credit facility bear interest at the Bloomberg Short-Term Bank Yield Index Rate plus an applicable margin. Interest rates as of December&#160;31, 2022 and 2021 were <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityInterestRateDuringPeriod" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="4" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">4.12</ix:nonFraction>% and <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityInterestRateDuringPeriod" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="4" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">2.91</ix:nonFraction>%, respectively. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company accounted for the Fourth amendment as both a debt modification and debt extinguishment in accordance with ASC 470, Debt (&#8220;ASC 470&#8221;). In connection with the Credit Agreement, the Company paid $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PaymentsOfDebtRestructuringCosts" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">2,027</ix:nonFraction> in fees. Of this amount, $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DeferredFinanceCostsNet" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">326</ix:nonFraction> was expensed and $<ix:nonFraction name="mntk:DebtIssuanceCostsCapitalized" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">1,701</ix:nonFraction> was capitalized and will be amortized over the life of the Credit Agreement. Amortized debt issuance expense in the amount of $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfFinancingCosts" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">412</ix:nonFraction>, $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfFinancingCosts" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">483</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfFinancingCosts" contextRef="P01_01_2020To12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">695</ix:nonFraction> for the <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">years ended December&#160;31, 2022, 2021 and 2020, respectively, was recorded in interest expense on the Statement of Operations. </div></div></div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As of December&#160;31, 2022, the Company was in compliance with all financial covenants related to the Credit Agreement. </div> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Annual Maturities of Long-Term Debt </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The following is a summary of annual principal maturities of long-term debt as of December&#160;31, 2022: </div> <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfMaturitiesOfLongTermDebtTableTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69698590"> <div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px;">&#160;</div>

<td>&#160;</td> </tr> </table> </ix:nonNumeric></ix:continuation><ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69698590"> </ix:continuation> <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxDisclosureTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69698301"> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">NOTE 14&#8212;INCOME TAXES </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company is subject to income taxes in the U.S. federal jurisdiction and various state and local jurisdictions. Tax regulations within each jurisdiction are subject to the interpretation of the related tax laws and regulations and require significant judgment to apply. </div> <div style="margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; font-size: 8pt;">&#160;</div> <div style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;text-align:center"><ix:exclude>-<div style="display:inline;">90</div>-</ix:exclude></div> </ix:nonNumeric></div> </div> <ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69698301" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698301_1"><ix:exclude><div style="margin-top:1em; margin-bottom:0em; page-break-before:always"></div></ix:exclude></ix:continuation> <ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698301_1" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698301_2"><ix:exclude><hr style="color:#999999;height:3px;width:100%"/></ix:exclude></ix:continuation><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698301_2" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698301_3"><ix:exclude> <div style="font-size: .83em;margin-top : 1.67em;margin-bottom : 1.67em;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div> </ix:exclude></ix:continuation> <div style="text-align:center"> <div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto"><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698301_3" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698301_4"> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">On March&#160;27, 2020, the Coronavirus Aid, Relief and Economic Security Act (the &#8220;CARES Act&#8221;) was signed into law. The CARES Act contains several corporate income tax provisions which include the (i)&#160;temporary removal of the </div></div><ix:nonFraction name="mntk:PercentageOfTaxableIncomeToUtilizeNetOperatingLossesWithdrawn" contextRef="P03_27_2020To03_27_2020" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">80</ix:nonFraction><div style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">% </div><div style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">taxable income limitation on utilization of Net Operating Losses (NOLs) generated before 2021, and (ii)&#160;temporarily liberalizing the interest deductions rules under IRC Sec.&#160;163(j) of the Tax Act raising the adjusted taxable income limitation from<div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div></div><ix:nonFraction name="mntk:PercentageOfAdjustedTaxableIncomeLimitation" contextRef="P01_01_2020To03_26_2020_CovidNineteenMemberMNTKUnexpectedEventAxis" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">30</ix:nonFraction><div style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">% to </div><ix:nonFraction name="mntk:PercentageOfAdjustedTaxableIncomeLimitation" contextRef="P03_27_2020To12_31_2020_CovidNineteenMemberMNTKUnexpectedEventAxis" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">50</ix:nonFraction><div style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">%, </div><div style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">among others. Additionally, on December&#160;27, 2020, the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021 (the &#8220;Appropriations Act&#8221;) was signed into law. Neither the CARES Act nor the Appropriations Act had a material impact on the Company&#8217;s financial statements for the years ending December&#160;2022, 2021 and 2020.</div></div></div> <div style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"></div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">On March&#160;11, 2021, the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021 (&#8220;ARPA&#8221;) was signed into law. The ARPA contains several corporate income tax provisions which include (i)&#160;extending the $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxReconciliationNondeductibleExpenseShareBasedCompensationCost" contextRef="P03_11_2021To03_11_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-6" scale="6" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">1</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million limitation on deductions for compensation paid to executives of publicly traded corporations to include compensation paid to the eight highest paid individuals (rather than three highest), plus the chief executive officer and the chief financial officer (effective for tax years after 2026), and (ii)&#160;extending the period for which companies may claim an employee retention credit. The ARPA did not have a <div style="text-indent: 0px; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">material impact on the Company&#8217;s financial statements for the years ending December 31, 2022 and 2021.</div> </div> <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfComponentsOfIncomeTaxExpenseBenefitTableTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69698593"><div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The following table details the components of the Company&#8217;s income tax provision (benefit) for the years ended December&#160;31, 2022, December&#160;31, 2021 and December&#160;31, 2020: </div></div></div> <div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;;text-indent: 0px;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-size: 12pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div></div>

<td style="white-space: nowrap; vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0); padding-bottom: 0.375pt; line-height: 1pt; font-size: 1pt;"> <div style="font-size: 1pt; line-height: 1pt;"><div style="font-size: 1pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="line-height: 1pt; font-size: 1pt;;font-weight:bold;display:inline;width:100%;">&#160;</div></div></div> </td> </tr> </table> </ix:continuation> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As of December&#160;31, 2022, the tax years <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxExaminationYearUnderExamination" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_InternalRevenueServiceIRSMemberusgaapIncomeTaxAuthorityNameAxis_TaxYear2018MemberusgaapTaxPeriodAxis">2018</ix:nonNumeric>, <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxExaminationYearUnderExamination" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_InternalRevenueServiceIRSMemberusgaapIncomeTaxAuthorityNameAxis_TaxYear2019MemberusgaapTaxPeriodAxis">2019</ix:nonNumeric> and <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxExaminationYearUnderExamination" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_InternalRevenueServiceIRSMemberusgaapIncomeTaxAuthorityNameAxis_TaxYear2020MemberusgaapTaxPeriodAxis">2020</ix:nonNumeric> are subject to examination by the IRS. </div> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Valuation Allowance </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company annually reviews its deferred tax assets for the possibility they will not be realized. A valuation allowance will be recorded if it is determined more than a 50% likelihood exists that a deferred tax asset will not be realized. A $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsValuationAllowance" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">3,950</ix:nonFraction> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;text-indent: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">valuation allowance exists, which represents the Company&#8217;s deferred tax assets that are not expected to be realized. This represents a </div></div> $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ValuationAllowanceDeferredTaxAssetChangeInAmount" contextRef="P01_01_2020To12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">50</ix:nonFraction><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div><div style="text-indent: 0px; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">increase in the valuation allowance from $</div><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsValuationAllowance" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">3,900</ix:nonFraction> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;text-indent: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">at the year ended December&#160;31, 2021. </div></div></div> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Uncertain Tax Position </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The calculation of the Company&#8217;s tax liabilities involves dealing with uncertainties in the application of complex tax laws and regulations in both federal and state jurisdictions. ASC 740 states that a tax benefit from an uncertain tax position may be recognized when it is more likely than not that the position will be sustained upon examination, including resolutions of any related appeals or litigation processes, on the basis of each situation&#8217;s technical merits. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">At this point in time the Company is not aware of any tax positions taken that would give rise to recording an uncertain tax position. As such, the Company has not recorded any liability for unrecognized tax benefits as of December&#160;31, 2022 or 2021. The Company records interest and penalties as a component of income tax expense. However, as there are <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:UnrecognizedTaxBenefits" contextRef="PAsOn12_21_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:UnrecognizedTaxBenefits" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen">no</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> unrecognized tax benefits for the years ended December&#160;31, 2022 and December&#160;31, 2021, the Company has <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:UnrecognizedTaxBenefitsIncomeTaxPenaltiesAndInterestAccrued" contextRef="PAsOn12_21_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:UnrecognizedTaxBenefitsIncomeTaxPenaltiesAndInterestAccrued" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen">zero</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> penalties or interest accrued at December&#160;31, 2022 and 2021, respectively. </div> </ix:continuation><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:DisclosureOfCompensationRelatedCostsShareBasedPaymentsTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69698212"> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">NOTE 15&#8212;SHARE-BASED COMPENSATION </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In January 2021, Montauk Renewables undertook the Reorganization Transactions which resulted in the Company owning all of the assets and entities (excluding Montauk USA) through which MNK&#8217;s business and operations were conducted. As a result of the Distribution, the options outstanding under MNK&#8217;s Employee Share Appreciation Rights Scheme (the &#8220;SAR Plan&#8221;) were cancelled. The Company recorded $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021_GeneralAndAdministrativeExpenseMemberusgaapIncomeStatementLocationAxis_StockAppreciationRightsSARSMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">2,050</ix:nonFraction> of accelerated compensation expense in its consolidated statements of operations within general and administrative expenses in connection with the cancellation of the options under the SAR Plan for the year ended December&#160;3<div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">1</div>, 2021. </div> <div style="margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; font-size: 8pt;">&#160;</div> <div style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;text-align:center"><ix:exclude>-9<div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">3</div>-</ix:exclude></div> </ix:nonNumeric></div> </div> <ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69698212" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698212_1"><ix:exclude><div style="margin-top:1em; margin-bottom:0em; page-break-before:always"></div></ix:exclude></ix:continuation> <ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698212_1" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698212_2"><ix:exclude><hr style="color:#999999;height:3px;width:100%"/></ix:exclude></ix:continuation><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698212_2" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698212_3"><ix:exclude> <div style="font-size: .83em;margin-top : 1.67em;margin-bottom : 1.67em;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div> </ix:exclude></ix:continuation> <div style="text-align:center"> <div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto"><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69698212_3" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698212_4"> <div style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The board of directors of Montauk Renewables adopted the Montauk Renewables, Inc. Equity and Incentive Compensation Plan (&#8220;MRI EICP&#8221;) in January 2021. Following the closing of the IPO, the board of directors of Montauk Renewables approved the grant of <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-qualified</div> stock options, restricted stock unit and restricted stock awards to the employees of Montauk Renewables and its subsidiaries in January 2021. In connection with the restricted stock grants the officers of the Company made elections under Section&#160;83(b) of the Code. Pursuant to such elections, the Company withheld <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:CommonStockCapitalSharesReservedForFutureIssuance" contextRef="PAsOn01_31_2021_MontaukRenewablesIncEquityAndIncentiveCompensationPlanMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_shares" decimals="INF" scale="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">950,214</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock from such awards at a price of $<ix:nonFraction name="mntk:StockOptionExercisePrice" contextRef="P01_31_2021To01_31_2021_MontaukRenewablesIncEquityAndIncentiveCompensationPlanMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD_per_Share" decimals="2" scale="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">11.38</ix:nonFraction> <div style="display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">per share from such awards. The Company records and reports restricted shares and restricted stock units when vested and in the case of options, when such awards are settled in the Company&#8217;s common stock. As of December&#160;31, 2022, unrecognized MRI EICP compensation expense for awards the Company expects to vest approximated </div></div> $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_MontaukRenewablesIncEquityAndIncentiveCompensationPlanMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">17,921</ix:nonFraction> and will be recognized over approximately <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardAwardVestingPeriod1" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_MontaukRenewablesIncEquityAndIncentiveCompensationPlanMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" format="ixt-sec:duryear">4</ix:nonNumeric> years. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In connection with a May 2021 asset acquisition, <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardMaximumNumberOfSharesPerEmployee" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_RestrictedStockMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_shares" decimals="INF" scale="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">1,250,000</ix:nonFraction> restricted stock awards (&#8220;RS Awards&#8221;) were granted to <ix:nonFraction name="mntk:NumberOfEmployees" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_RestrictedStockMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_Employees" decimals="0" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen">two</ix:nonFraction> employees that were hired by the Company in connection with such acquisition. The RS Awards were to vest over a five-year period and subject to the achievement of time and performance-based vesting criteria over such period. In May 2022, the RS Awards were amended to remove the performance-based vesting criteria and will only be subject to time-based vesting requirements over a five-year period. The awards were revalued at $<ix:nonFraction name="mntk:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsRevaluedAmount" contextRef="P05_31_2022To05_31_2022_TimeBasedRestrictedStockAwardsMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">15,500</ix:nonFraction>. Stock compensation expense related to the two awards was $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_TimeBasedRestrictedStockAwardsMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">2,863</ix:nonFraction> for the year ended December&#160;31, 2022. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The restricted stock, restricted stock unit<div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">s</div> and option awards are subject to vesting schedules that commence or conclude, in the case of the option and restricted stock unit awards, on the <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">one-year</div> anniversary of the grant date and are subject to the terms and conditions of the MRI EICP and related award agreements including, in the case of the restricted stock awards, each officer having made an election under Section&#160;83(b) of the Code. The Company recorded $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_GeneralAndAdministrativeExpenseMemberusgaapIncomeStatementLocationAxis_MontaukRenewablesIncEquityAndIncentiveCompensationPlanMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis_SectionEightyThreeBMemberMNTKElectionCodeAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">10,813</ix:nonFraction> of compensation expense in its consolidated statements of operations within general and administrative expenses for the year ended December&#160;31, 2021 in connection with the withheld <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:CommonStockCapitalSharesReservedForFutureIssuance" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2021_MontaukRenewablesIncEquityAndIncentiveCompensationPlanMemberusgaapAwardTypeAxis_SectionEightyThreeBMemberMNTKElectionCodeAxis" unitRef="Unit_shares" decimals="INF" scale="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">950,214</ix:nonFraction> shares associated with the Section&#160;83(b) elections. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Options granted under the MRI EICP allow the recipient to receive the Company&#8217;s common stock equal to the appreciation in the fair market value of the Company&#8217;s common stock between the grant date and the exercise and settlement of options into shares as of the exercise date(s). <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfShareBasedPaymentAwardStockOptionsValuationAssumptionsTableTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69698213">The fair value of the MRI EICP options was estimated using the Black-Scholes option pricing model with the following weighted-average assumptions (no dividends were expected):</ix:nonNumeric> </div> <ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69698213"> <div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px;">&#160;</div>

The Company matches <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanEmployerMatchingContributionPercentOfMatch" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">50</ix:nonFraction>% of an employee&#8217;s deferrals up to <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanEmployerMatchingContributionPercent" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">4</ix:nonFraction>%. The Company also contributes <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanMaximumAnnualContributionsPerEmployeePercent" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_SafeHarborContributionMemberusgaapRetirementPlanNameAxis" unitRef="Unit_pure" decimals="2" scale="-2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">3</ix:nonFraction>% of eligible employee&#8217;s compensation expense as a safe harbor contribution. The matching contributions vest ratably over <ix:nonNumeric name="mntk:DefinedContributionPlanEmployerMatchingContributionVestingPeriod" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_SafeHarborContributionMemberusgaapRetirementPlanNameAxis" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen">four years</ix:nonNumeric> of service, while the safe harbor contributions vest immediately. Incurred expense related to the 401(k) plan was approximately $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanCostRecognized" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">589</ix:nonFraction>, $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanCostRecognized" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">502</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanCostRecognized" contextRef="P01_01_2020To12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">521</ix:nonFraction> for the years ended December&#160;31, 2022, 2021 and 2020, respectively. </div> </ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:RelatedPartyTransactionsDisclosureTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69697723"> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">NOTE 17&#8212;RELATED-PARTY TRANSACTIONS </div></div> <div style="margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Related Party Loan </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">On January&#160;26, 2021, the Company entered into a Loan Agreement and Secured Promissory Note (the &#8220;Initial Promissory Note&#8221;) with MNK. MNK is currently an affiliate of the Company and certain of the Company&#8217;s directors and executive officers are also directors and executive officers of MNK. Pursuant to the Initial Promissory Note, the Company advanced a cash loan of $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:OriginationOfNotesReceivableFromRelatedParties" contextRef="P01_26_2021To01_26_2021_LoansAdvancedToRelatedPartiesMemberusgaapRelatedPartyTransactionsByRelatedPartyAxis_MnkMemberusgaapRelatedPartyTransactionAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">5,000</ix:nonFraction> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"></div></div><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">to MNK </div></div><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"> for MNK to pay its dividends tax liability arising from the Reorganization Transactions under the South African Income Tax Act, 1962 (Act&#160;No.&#160;58 of 1962), as amended (the &#8220;South African Income Tax Act&#8221;). On February 22, 2021, the Company and MNK entered into an Amended and Restated Promissory Note (the &#8220;Amended Promissory Note&#8221;) to increase the principal amount of the loan to a total of $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:NotesReceivableRelatedPartiesCurrent" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">7,140</ix:nonFraction>, in the aggregate, and on December 22, 2021 entered into the Second Amended and Restated Loan Agreement and Secured Promissory Note (the &#8220;Second Amended Promissory Note&#8221;) to increase the principal amount of the loan to a total of </div></div> $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:NotesReceivableRelatedPartiesCurrent" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">8,940</ix:nonFraction>, <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">in the aggregate, and on December 22, 2022 entered into the First Amendment of the Second Amended and Restated Loan Agreement and Secured Promissory Note (the &#8220;First Amendment of Second Amended Promissory Note&#8221;) to amend the maturity date of June 30, 2023, each in accordance with the Company&#8217;s obligations set forth in the Transaction Implementation Agreement entered into by and among the Company, MNK and the other party thereto, dated November 6, 2020, and amended on January 14, 2021. The terms of the Amended Promissory Note provide the </div></div></div> <div style="background: none; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none; text-decoration: none;;display:inline;"> </div></div> <div style="font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-size: 8pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><ix:exclude>&#160;</ix:exclude></div></div></div> <div style="text-align: center; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; page-break-before: always;"></div></ix:exclude> </ix:nonNumeric></div> </div> <ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69697723" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69697723_1"><ix:exclude><hr style="width: 100%; height: 3px; color: rgb(153, 153, 153);"/></ix:exclude></ix:continuation><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69697723_1" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69697723_2"><ix:exclude> <div style="font-size: 13.28px; margin-top: 1.67em; margin-bottom: 1.67em;;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc"><span style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Table of Contents</span></a></div> </ix:exclude></ix:continuation> <div style="text-align:center"> <div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto"><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69697723_2" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69697723_3"> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;;text-indent: 0px;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Company a security </div></div> interest <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">of</div>&#160;<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtConversionConvertedInstrumentSharesIssued1" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_shares" decimals="INF" scale="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">800,000</ix:nonFraction> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">shares of the Company and require MNK to use the proceeds of any such sale of the shares to repay the note. The Amended Promissory Note also has default provisions where MNK will deliver any unsold shares of the Company back to the Company to satisfy repayment of the note. The Amended Promissory Note matures on June 30, 2023. </div></div></div> </ix:continuation></div> </div> </div> </div> </div> <div> <div> <div style="background-color:white;display: inline;"> <div style="text-align:center"> <div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto"><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69697723_3"> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Under applicable guidance for variable interest entities in ASC 810, Consolidation, the Company determined that MNK is a variable interest entity. The Company concluded that it is not the primary beneficiary of the variable interest entity, as the Company does not have a controlling financial interest and does not have the power to direct the activities that most significantly impact the economic performance of MNK. Accordingly, the Company concluded that presentation of the Amended Promissory Note as a related party receivable remains appropriate. </div></div></div> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Related Party Reimbursements </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Periodically the Company will reimburse MNK and HCI Managerial Services Proprietary Limited, the administrator for the Company&#8217;s secondarily listed Johannesburg Stock Exchange trading symbol, for expenses incurred on behalf of the Company. Amounts reimbursed were $<ix:nonFraction name="mntk:ReimbursementsReceivedFromRelatedParty" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022_MnkAndHoskenConsolidatedInvestmentsLimitedMemberusgaapRelatedPartyTransactionAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">68</ix:nonFraction>, $<ix:nonFraction name="mntk:ReimbursementsReceivedFromRelatedParty" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021_MnkAndHoskenConsolidatedInvestmentsLimitedMemberusgaapRelatedPartyTransactionAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">813</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction name="mntk:ReimbursementsReceivedFromRelatedParty" contextRef="P01_01_2020To12_31_2020_MnkAndHoskenConsolidatedInvestmentsLimitedMemberusgaapRelatedPartyTransactionAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">238</ix:nonFraction> for the years ended December&#160;31, 2022, 2021 and 2020, respectively. $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DueToRelatedPartiesCurrentAndNoncurrent" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2022_MnkAndHoskenConsolidatedInvestmentsLimitedMemberusgaapRelatedPartyTransactionAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">26</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DueToRelatedPartiesCurrentAndNoncurrent" contextRef="PAsOn12_31_2021_MnkAndHoskenConsolidatedInvestmentsLimitedMemberusgaapRelatedPartyTransactionAxis" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">0</ix:nonFraction> were owed as of December&#160;31, 2022 and 2021, respectively. </div> </ix:continuation> <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:SegmentReportingDisclosureTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69698306"> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">NOTE 18&#8212;SEGMENT INFORMATION </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company&#8217;s reportable segments for the years ended December&#160;31, 2022, 2021, and 2020 are Renewable Natural Gas and Renewable Electricity Generation. Renewable Natural Gas includes the production of RNG. Renewable Electricity Generation includes generation of electricity at <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">biogas-to-electricity</div></div> plants. The corporate entity is not determined to be an operating segment but is discretely disclosed for purposes of reconciliation of the Company&#8217;s consolidated financial statements. <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ReconciliationOfOperatingProfitLossFromSegmentsToConsolidatedTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69698297">The following table is consistent with the manner in which the chief operating decision maker evaluates the performance of each segment and allocates the Company&#8217;s resources. In the following tables &#8220;RNG&#8221; refers to Renewable Natural Gas and &#8220;REG&#8221; refer to Renewable Electricity Generation. </ix:nonNumeric></div> <ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69698297" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69698297_1"><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px;">&#160;</div>

<td style="white-space:nowrap;vertical-align:bottom">%&#160;</td> </tr> </table> </ix:nonNumeric></ix:continuation> <ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:LeasesOfLesseeDisclosureTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69697771"> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"></div></div></div> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"></div></div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;;text-indent: 0px;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">NOTE 19&#8212;LEASES </div></div></div></div> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The Company leases office space and other office equipment under operating lease arrangements (with initial terms greater than twelve months), expiring in various years through 2033. These leases have been entered into to better enable the Company to conduct business operations. Office space is leased to provide adequate workspace for all employees in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania and Houston, Texas. Office space and office equipment agreements that exceed 12 months are accounted for as operating leases in accordance with ASC 842, Leases. </div></div></div> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The Company also leases safety equipment for the various operational sites in the United States. The term of certain equipment exceeds twelve months and is accordingly classified as a finance lease under ASC 842. The finance leases expire in 2024 and were entered into in order to provide a safe work environment for operational employees. </div></div></div> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The Company determines if an arrangement is, or contains, a lease at inception based on whether that contract conveys the right to control the use of an identified asset in exchange for consideration for a period of </div></div></div> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none; text-decoration: none;;display:inline;"> </div></div> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none; text-decoration: none;;display:inline;"> </div></div> <div style="margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; font-size: 8pt;"><ix:exclude>&#160;</ix:exclude></div> <div style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;text-align:center"><ix:exclude>-<div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">98</div>-</ix:exclude></div> </ix:nonNumeric></div> </div> </div> <ix:continuation id="TextSelection_69697771" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69697771_1"><div></div></ix:continuation> </div> <ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69697771_1" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69697771_2"><div> <div style="background-color:white;display: inline;"> <ix:exclude><div style="margin-top:1em; margin-bottom:0em; page-break-before:always"></div></ix:exclude> <ix:exclude><hr style="color:#999999;height:3px;width:100%"/></ix:exclude><ix:exclude> <div style="font-size: .83em;margin-top : 1.67em;margin-bottom : 1.67em;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div> </ix:exclude></div> </div></ix:continuation> </div> <div> <div style="background-color:white;display: inline;"> <div style="text-align:center"> <div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto"><ix:continuation id="TextSelectionAppend_69697771_2" continuedAt="TextSelectionAppend_69697771_3"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;;text-indent: 0px;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">time. For all operating lease arrangements, the Company presents at the commencement date: a lease liability, which is a lessee&#8217;s obligation to make lease payments arising from a lease, measured on a discounted basis; and a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">right-of-use</div></div> asset, which is an asset that represents the lessee&#8217;s right to use, or control the use of, a specified asset for the lease term. </div></div> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"></div> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The Company has elected, as a practical expedient, not to separate <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-lease</div> components from lease components, and instead account for each separate component as a single lease component for all lease arrangements, as lessee. In addition, the Company has elected, as a practical expedient, not to apply lease recognition requirements to short-term lease arrangements, generally those with a lease term of less than twelve months, for all classes of underlying assets. In determination of the lease term, the Company considers the likelihood of lease renewal options and lease termination provisions. </div></div> <div style="text-indent: 4%; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;text-indent: 0px;;display:inline;">The Company uses its incremental borrowing rate, as the basis to calculate the present value of future lease payments, at lease commencement. The incremental borrowing rate represents the rate of interest a lessee would have to pay to borrow an amount equal to the total lease payments on a collateralized basis over a similar term in a similar economic environment.</div></div></div> <div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none;;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px; background: none; text-decoration: none;;display:inline;"> </div></div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As of December&#160;31, 2022, there were no leases entered into which have not yet commenced and that would entitle the Company to significant rights or create additional obligations. The total lease cost included in our consolidated financial statements statement of operations for the years ended December&#160;31, 2022, 2021 and 2020 were $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LeaseCost" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">412</ix:nonFraction>, $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LeaseCost" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">349</ix:nonFraction>, and $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LeaseCost" contextRef="P01_01_2020To12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">322</ix:nonFraction>, respectively. </div> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Leases with an initial term of 12 months or less are not recorded on the Consolidated Balance Sheet and the lease expense for those leases is recognized on a straight-line basis. The short-term lease expense for the years ended December&#160;31, 2022, 2021 and 2020 were approximately $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShortTermLeaseCost" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">895</ix:nonFraction>, $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShortTermLeaseCost" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">494</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShortTermLeaseCost" contextRef="P01_01_2020To12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit_USD" decimals="-3" scale="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">637</ix:nonFraction>, respectively. </div> <ix:nonNumeric name="mntk:DisclosureOfSupplementalInformationRelatedToOperatingLeasesTableTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true"> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Supplemental information related to operating lease arrangements was as follows as of and for the year ended December&#160;31, 2022 and 2021: </div> <div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px;">&#160;</div>

<td style="white-space:nowrap;vertical-align:bottom">)&#160;</td> </tr> </table> </ix:nonNumeric> <div style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As a result of incurring a net loss for the year ended December&#160;31, 2021, <ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AntidilutiveSecuritiesExcludedFromComputationOfEarningsPerShareAmount" contextRef="P01_01_2021To12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit_shares" decimals="0" scale="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal">3,897,811</ix:nonFraction> potential antidilutive shares were excluded from the above loss per share calculation. </div> </ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:SubsequentEventsTextBlock" contextRef="P01_01_2022To12_31_2022" escape="true" continuedAt="TextSelection_69697757"> <div style="margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">NOTE&#160;23&#8212;SUBSEQUENT EVENTS </div></div> <div style="margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Subsequent Events </div></div> <div style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company evaluated its December&#160;31, 2022 consolidated financial statements through the date the financial statements were issued. <td style="width:11%;vertical-align:top" align="left" id="rom376707_15"><span style="font-weight:bold">ITEM&#160;9A.</span></td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold;text-align:left">CONTROLS AND PROCEDURES. </p></td></tr></table> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Management&#8217;s Evaluation of Disclosure Controls and Procedures. </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">We maintain disclosure controls and procedures that are designed to ensure that information required to be disclosed in the Company&#8217;s reports under the Exchange Act is recorded, processed, summarized and reported within the time periods specified in the SEC&#8217;s rules and forms and that such information is accumulated and communicated to the Company&#8217;s management, including the Company&#8217;s Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, as appropriate, to allow timely decisions regarding required disclosures. Any controls and procedures, no matter how well designed and operated, can provide only reasonable assurance of achieving the desired control objectives. The Company, with the participation of the Company&#8217;s Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, concluded that, as of December&#160;31, 2022 (the end of the period covered by this Annual Report on Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">10-K),</span> the Company&#8217;s disclosure controls and procedures were effective, pursuant to Rule <span style="white-space:nowrap">13a-15</span> and Rule <span style="white-space:nowrap">15d-15</span> of the Exchange Act. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Management&#8217;s Annual Report on Internal Control Over Financial Reporting. </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Our management is responsible for establishing and maintaining adequate internal control over financial reporting as defined in Rule <span style="white-space:nowrap">13a-15(f)</span> under the Exchange Act. Our internal control over financial reporting is designed to provide reasonable, but not absolute, assurance regarding the reliability of financial reporting and the preparation of financial statements for external purposes in accordance with GAAP and includes those policies and procedures that pertain to the maintenance of records that, in reasonable detail, accurately and fairly reflect the transactions and dispositions of our assets; provide reasonable assurance that transactions are recorded as necessary to permit preparation of financial statements in accordance with GAAP, and that our receipts and expenditures are being made only in accordance with appropriate authorizations; and provide reasonable assurance regarding prevention or timely detection of unauthorized acquisition, use, or disposition of our assets that could have a material effect on our financial statements. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Our management has conducted an evaluation of the effectiveness of our internal control over financial reporting, using the criteria set forth by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission in Internal Control&#8212;Integrated Framework (2013) (&#8220;COSO&#8221;). Based on the results of this evaluation, management concluded that our internal control over financial reporting was effective as of December&#160;31, 2022. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">This Annual Report on Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">10-K</span> does not include an attestation report of internal controls from our independent registered public accounting firm due to our status as an emerging growth company under the JOBS Act. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Changes in Internal Control over Financial Reporting. </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">There have been no material changes in our internal control over financial reporting during the quarter ended December&#160;31, 2022, that have materially affected, or are reasonably likely to materially affect, our internal control over financial reporting </p> <p style="font-size:18pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
